title: LOW: No replay protection beyond Date freshness window
description:
HTTP Signature verification in inbox.rs has no replay protection mechanism beyond the 12-hour Date header freshness check. The exact same signed request can be replayed an unlimited number of times within the 12-hour window.

Most ActivityPub implementations (Mastodon included) also lack per-request replay protection, so this is consistent with the ecosystem. However, it means a network-level attacker who captures a signed Follow or Create activity can replay it repeatedly within the window.

The impact is limited because:
1. INSERT OR IGNORE is used for most state-changing operations (followers, posts), so replays are idempotent
2. The 12-hour window is relatively short
3. Requires network-level interception

This is noted as LOW because it matches the ecosystem norm and the idempotent writes make replay mostly harmless. A future improvement would be to maintain a short-lived seen-signatures cache (keyed on SHA-256 of the signature bytes, TTL = 12 hours).

File: src/inbox.rs verify_and_fetch_actor()
close_reason: Acceptable: inbox processing is idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE). Replay within 12h window produces no side effects.
